# sovereign-vault

> Reversible PII tokenization for LLM pipelines

## What it does
Replaces PII with stable HMAC-bound placeholder tokens. Cloud AI receives
tokenized text and reasons about patterns without seeing real values.
Original values are reconstructed locally after the cloud response.

## Use cases
- HIPAA-compliant medical document analysis with cloud LLMs
- GDPR-compliant customer data processing via AI pipelines
- Legal e-discovery: send case documents to AI without exposing party identities
- Financial fraud detection: pattern analysis without raw account numbers
- Multi-agent AI: pass sensitive context between agents safely

## Key concepts
- Reversible tokenization: unlike redaction, original values can be reconstructed
- HMAC-bound tokens: same value always maps to same token within a session
- Session-scoped: no cross-session leakage
- RAM-only: no vault state written to disk
- Three detection layers: regex, GLiNER NER, Ollama contextual sweep
- STRICT mode: fail if any token missing from reconstruction
- LENIENT mode: allow partial reconstruction

## Install
pip install sovereign-vault
pip install sovereign-vault[ner]
pip install sovereign-vault[all]

## Quick use
from sovereign_vault import VaultSession
with VaultSession() as vault:
    abstract = vault.tokenize("John Doe SSN 123-45-6789 called jane@firm.com")
    response = my_llm(abstract)
    real_response = vault.reconstruct(response)

## Links
- GitHub: https://github.com/brokenbartender/sovereign-vault
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/sovereign-vault/
- Homepage: https://lexipro.online
